speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Give me some music—music, moody food

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Of us that trade in love.

speaker: ALL
text: The music, ho!

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Let it alone. Let’s to billiards. Come, Charmian.

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: My arm is sore. Best play with Mardian.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: As well a woman with an eunuch played

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: As with a woman.—Come, you’ll play with me, sir?

speaker: MARDIAN
text: As well as I can, madam.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: And when good will is showed, though ’t come too short,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: The actor may plead pardon. I’ll none now.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Give me mine angle; we’ll to th’ river. There,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: My music playing far off, I will betray

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Tawny-finned fishes. My bended hook shall pierce

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Their slimy jaws, and as I draw them up

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: I’ll think them every one an Antony

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: And say “Aha! You’re caught.”

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: ’Twas merry when

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: You wagered on your angling; when your diver

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: Did hang a salt fish on his hook, which he

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: With fervency drew up.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: That time?—O, times!—

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: I laughed him out of patience; and that night

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: I laughed him into patience; and next morn,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Ere the ninth hour, I drunk him to his bed,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Then put my tires and mantles on him, whilst

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: I wore his sword Philippan.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: O, from Italy!

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Ram thou thy fruitful tidings in mine ears,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: That long time have been barren.

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Madam, madam—

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Antonio’s dead! If thou say so, villain,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Thou kill’st thy mistress. But well and free,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: If thou so yield him, there is gold, and here

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: My bluest veins to kiss, a hand that kings

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Have lipped and trembled kissing.

speaker: MESSENGER
text: First, madam, he is well.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Why, there’s more gold. But sirrah, mark, we use

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: To say the dead are well. Bring it to that,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: The gold I give thee will I melt and pour

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Down thy ill-uttering throat.

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Good madam, hear me.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Well, go to, I will.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: But there’s no goodness in thy face—if Antony

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Be free and healthful, so tart a favor

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: To trumpet such good tidings! If not well,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Thou shouldst come like a Fury crowned with snakes,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Not like a formal man.

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Will ’t please you hear me?

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: I have a mind to strike thee ere thou speak’st.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Yet if thou say Antony lives, is well,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Or friends with Caesar or not captive to him,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: I’ll set thee in a shower of gold and hail

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Rich pearls upon thee.

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Madam, he’s well.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Well said.

speaker: MESSENGER
text: And friends with Caesar.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Th’ art an honest man.

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Caesar and he are greater friends than ever.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Make thee a fortune from me.

speaker: MESSENGER
text: But yet, madam—

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: I do not like “But yet.” It does allay

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: The good precedence. Fie upon “But yet.”

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: “But yet” is as a jailer to bring forth

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Some monstrous malefactor. Prithee, friend,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Pour out the pack of matter to mine ear,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: The good and bad together: he’s friends with Caesar,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: In state of health, thou say’st, and, thou say’st, free.

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Free, madam, no. I made no such report.

speaker: MESSENGER
text: He’s bound unto Octavia.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: For what good turn?

speaker: MESSENGER
text: For the best turn i’ th’ bed.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: I am pale, Charmian.

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Madam, he’s married to Octavia.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: The most infectious pestilence upon thee!

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Good madam, patience!

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: What say you?

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Hence, horrible villain, or I’ll spurn thine eyes

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Like balls before me! I’ll unhair thy head!

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Thou shalt be whipped with wire and stewed in brine,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Smarting in ling’ring pickle.

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Gracious madam,

speaker: MESSENGER
text: I that do bring the news made not the match.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Say ’tis not so, a province I will give thee

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: And make thy fortunes proud. The blow thou hadst

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Shall make thy peace for moving me to rage,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: And I will boot thee with what gift beside

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Thy modesty can beg.

speaker: MESSENGER
text: He’s married, madam.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Rogue, thou hast lived too long.

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Nay then, I’ll run.

speaker: MESSENGER
text: What mean you, madam? I have made no fault.

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: Good madam, keep yourself within yourself.

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: The man is innocent.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Some innocents ’scape not the thunderbolt.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Melt Egypt into Nile, and kindly creatures

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Turn all to serpents! Call the slave again.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Though I am mad, I will not bite him. Call!

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: He is afeard to come.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: I will not hurt him.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: These hands do lack nobility that they strike

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: A meaner than myself, since I myself

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Have given myself the cause.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Come hither, sir.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Though it be honest, it is never good

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: To bring bad news. Give to a gracious message

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: An host of tongues, but let ill tidings tell

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Themselves when they be felt.

speaker: MESSENGER
text: I have done my duty.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Is he married?

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: I cannot hate thee worser than I do

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: If thou again say “yes.”

speaker: MESSENGER
text: He’s married, madam.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: The gods confound thee! Dost thou hold there still?

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Should I lie, madam?

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: O, I would thou didst,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: So half my Egypt were submerged and made

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: A cistern for scaled snakes! Go, get thee hence.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Hadst thou Narcissus in thy face, to me

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Thou wouldst appear most ugly. He is married?

speaker: MESSENGER
text: I crave your Highness’ pardon.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: He is married?

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Take no offense that I would not offend you.

speaker: MESSENGER
text: To punish me for what you make me do

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Seems much unequal. He’s married to Octavia.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: O, that his fault should make a knave of thee

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: That art not what th’ art sure of! Get thee hence.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: The merchandise which thou hast brought from Rome

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Are all too dear for me. Lie they upon thy hand,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: And be undone by ’em!

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: Good your Highness, patience.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: In praising Antony, I have dispraised Caesar.

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: Many times, madam.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: I am paid for ’t now. Lead me from hence;

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: I faint. O, Iras, Charmian! ’Tis no matter.—

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Go to the fellow, good Alexas. Bid him

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Report the feature of Octavia, her years,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Her inclination; let him not leave out

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: The color of her hair. Bring me word quickly.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Let him forever go—let him not, Charmian.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Though he be painted one way like a Gorgon,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text:
The other way ’s a Mars.
                 
                (
                To
                 
                Mardian
                .
                )
               Bid you Alexas

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Bring me word how tall she is.—Pity me, Charmian,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: But do not speak to me. Lead me to my chamber.
